claims.claim: C-CRP v3 ranks first against eight official-code-level baselines on Sports, Toys, Home, and Tools under 10k-user/101-candidate same-candidate evaluation, with all per-domain paired Holm tests positive and significant.
claims.allowed_wording: C-CRP v3 ranks first in the four tested same-candidate new-domain certificates against eight official-code-level baselines.
claims.forbidden_wording: full-catalog SOTA; universal recommender SOTA; paper-ready SOTA without scoped modules and manuscript review
claims.notes: On Sports, Toys, Home, and Tools 10k-user/101-candidate same-candidate domains, C-CRP v3 ranks first against eight official-code-level baselines, with all 56 per-domain C-CRP-vs-official metric tests positive and Holm-significant.

claims.claim: The main comparison ledger contains 32 official baseline rows and 4 C-CRP rows with complete full-metric, row-count, score-coverage, provenance, and local/server evidence checks.
claims.allowed_wording: The paper-facing ledger is complete for the declared four-domain same-candidate comparison.
claims.forbidden_wording: treating the ledger alone as a whole-paper readiness verdict

claims.claim: C-CRP event-level uncertainty stratifies ranking reliability: high-uncertainty bins have lower NDCG@10, MRR, and HR@10 than low-uncertainty bins in all four domains.
claims.allowed_wording: Use as descriptive motivation evidence that C-CRP uncertainty stratifies same-candidate ranking reliability.
claims.forbidden_wording: causal uncertainty effect; statistically significant bin effect; exhaustive baseline calibration evidence

claims.claim: The four-domain component ablation is package-ready and supports a diagnostic discussion of weak, redundant, or mixed C-CRP components.
claims.allowed_wording: Report leave-one-component-out diagnostics; several components are neutral, weak, redundant, or mixed.
claims.forbidden_wording: every component is necessary; every uncertainty term improves performance; component effects are statistically significant

claims.claim: Every nontrivial C-CRP component is necessary for the observed gains.
claims.forbidden_wording: necessary; essential; each component contributes; removing any component hurts
claims.notes: Positive or neutral removal deltas directly contradict a strong necessity claim.

claims.claim: The hyperparameter module supports NDCG@10 stability/sensitivity for eta and the uncertainty weight grid across four domains under validation-only selection and reporting-only test sweeps.
claims.allowed_wording: Eta and weight-grid choices are stable within the preregistered tolerance on NDCG@10 across the four domains.
claims.forbidden_wording: all-metric robustness; universal optimum; test-selected tuning; main-table SOTA evidence

claims.claim: A positive risk exponent or risk penalty is necessary or uniformly beneficial.
claims.allowed_wording: Do not claim risk-penalty necessity; discuss eta as stable/sensitivity-only.
claims.forbidden_wording: eta must be positive; risk penalty is necessary; risk penalty uniformly improves

claims.claim: The framework overview figure is ready to describe the controlled same-candidate pipeline and evidence gates.
claims.allowed_wording: Use the figure to explain the pipeline and where uncertainty/calibration/risk-adjusted ranking enter.
claims.forbidden_wording: using the figure as evidence that observation, ablation, or hyperparameter modules are complete

claims.allowed_wording: Evidence is ready for strict paper drafting and subsequent manuscript-level claim/citation review.
claims.forbidden_wording: submission-ready; final READY; cleared for camera-ready

claims.claim: The method is a full-catalog or universal recommender SOTA system.
claims.allowed_wording: Controlled same-candidate ranking/reranking reliability under the tested protocol.
claims.forbidden_wording: full-catalog SOTA; universal cross-domain winner beyond tested same-candidate domains
claims.notes: The certificate explicitly excludes full-catalog and universal SOTA claims.

must_add_categories: LLM-based recommendation and sequential recommendation foundations
uncertainty estimation, calibration, and selective/risk-aware prediction
all eight official baselines used in the main same-candidate table
same-candidate/reranking evaluation protocol and paired significance testing
recent 2025-2026 LLM recommender and uncertainty-aware recommender work
notes: This audit does not fabricate BibTeX entries.
Run a full citation audit after a manuscript and .bib file exist.

red_flag_terms.state-of-the-art: Allowed only with controlled same-candidate scope; forbidden for full-catalog or universal claims.
red_flag_terms.significant: Allowed for the paired official comparison only where Holm-significant tests are cited.
red_flag_terms.necessary: Forbidden for all C-CRP components and positive eta/risk penalty.
red_flag_terms.robust: Use stability/sensitivity for NDCG@10 controls, not broad robustness.
red_flag_terms.first: Blocked until a literature-backed novelty and citation audit exists.

next_actions: Draft the paper using only SUPPORTED allowed wording and the explicit forbidden-wording guardrails.
Run ARIS paper-claim-audit and aris-citation-audit on the existing manuscript and bibliography, then revise text to the allowed wording in this audit.
Run GPT-5.5/Codex review on each written section before calling the paper submission-ready.
